articles.title: Flagg Edges Knueppel in Tight Rookie of the Year Vote
articles.original_title: Flagg edges Knueppel in tight Rookie of Year vote
articles.summary: Mavericks rookie Cooper Flagg secured the NBA Rookie of the Year award after a season in which he broke multiple records for a teenage player. He narrowly defeated teammate Knueppel in the voting, highlighting his impact on Dallas' young core.
key_points: Cooper Flagg won Rookie of the Year, edging out Knueppel in a close vote.
Flagg set several teenage rookie records during the season.
The award underscores Flagg's growing role with the Mavericks.

articles.title: Magic Grind Out Game 4 Win, Push Pistons to Brink
articles.original_title: Magic grind out Game 4 win, push Pistons to brink
articles.summary: The Orlando Magic defeated the Detroit Pistons in Game 4, extending their series lead and putting Detroit on the edge of elimination. The win came after a hard‑fought battle, with Orlando capitalizing on late‑game execution.
key_points: Orlando won Game 4, taking a series advantage over Detroit.
The victory puts the Pistons close to being eliminated.
Key performances from the Magic's core helped secure the win.

articles.title: Nuggets Rule Gordon Out for Pivotal Game 5
articles.original_title: Nuggets rule Gordon out for pivotal Game 5
articles.summary: Denver announced that forward Aaron Gordon will miss Game 5 due to lingering left hamstring tightness. His absence comes at a critical juncture in the series, forcing the Nuggets to adjust their rotation.
key_points: Aaron Gordon is sidelined for Game 5 with a left hamstring issue.
The injury is classified as tightness, not a major tear.
Denver will need to reconfigure its frontcourt for the upcoming game.

articles.title: Riley Confirms He’s Not Retiring, Sticks With ‘Win Big’ Philosophy
articles.original_title: Riley not retiring, sticking with 'win big' approach
articles.summary: Miami Heat President Pat Riley clarified that he has no plans to retire or alter his aggressive "win big" approach, despite the team missing the playoffs for the first time since the 2018‑19 season. Riley emphasized continuity in the organization’s culture and future direction.
key_points: Pat Riley says he will not retire and will maintain his competitive philosophy.
The Heat missed the playoffs for the first time since 2018‑19.
Riley stresses staying the course rather than making drastic changes.

articles.title: Lakers Guards Smart, Kennard Fined After Verbal Spat with Officials
articles.original_title: Smart, Kennard fined for verbal spat with officials
articles.summary: Marcus Smart and Luke Kennard were each fined by the NBA for a heated exchange with referees following the Lakers' 115‑96 loss to the Houston Rockets in Game 4. The league cited unsportsmanlike conduct during the post‑game confrontation.
key_points: Both Smart and Kennard received fines for arguing with officials.
The incident occurred after a 115‑96 Game 4 loss to the Rockets.
NBA officials emphasized the importance of respectful communication with referees.

articles.title: Wembanyama Returns with 27 Points, Spurs Take 3‑1 Lead
articles.original_title: Wemby back, unhappy with concussion process
articles.summary: Victor Wembanyama scored 27 points, grabbed 12 rebounds and blocked seven shots in his first game back after a concussion. His performance helped the San Antonio Spurs defeat the Portland Trail Blazers, extending the series lead to 3‑1.
key_points: Wembanyama posted a near‑triple‑double with 27‑12‑7 in his return.
Spurs won the game, moving the series to a 3‑1 advantage over Portland.
Wembanyama expressed dissatisfaction with the league’s concussion protocol.

Chicago Bulls are evaluating several candidates for a permanent role.
New Orleans Pelicans remain without a head coach, focusing on long‑term fit.

articles.title: Federal Prosecutors to File New Charges Against Terry Rozier
articles.original_title: Feds: Plan to file new charges against Rozier
articles.summary: U.S. prosecutors announced plans to file superseding charges of sports bribery and honest‑services wire fraud against NBA guard Terry Rozier. The new indictment follows earlier allegations related to gambling activities.
key_points: Charges to include sports bribery and honest‑services wire fraud.
Prosecutors intend to file a superseding indictment against Rozier.
The case is being handled by a federal assistant U.S. attorney.

articles.title: Bill Simmons Proposes Veteran‑Heavy Superteam Centered on LeBron James
articles.original_title: Bill Simmons creates super team of aging veterans led by LeBron James - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Sports media figure Bill Simmons outlined a hypothetical NBA superteam built around LeBron James and composed primarily of aging veterans. The concept emphasizes experience and championship pedigree over youth and athleticism. The proposal is speculative and intended as a discussion piece rather than an actual roster move.
key_points: LeBron James is positioned as the franchise cornerstone of the imagined squad.
The roster would feature veteran players past their prime but with multiple rings.
The article is a thought‑experiment, not a report of real negotiations.

articles.title: Luka Dončić Pledges Money to Adou Thiero After Game‑4 Ejection
articles.original_title: Lakers’ Luka Doncic makes financial pledge to Adou Thiero after embarrassing Game 4 ejection - New York Post
articles.summary: According to the New York Post, Dallas Mavericks guard Luka Dončić promised a financial contribution to Adou Thiero following an embarrassing ejection in Game 4 of a recent playoff series. The pledge was presented as a gesture of goodwill after the incident, though details of the amount were not disclosed.
key_points: Dončić was ejected in Game 4, prompting criticism and media attention.
He offered a monetary pledge to Thiero as a conciliatory gesture.
The story focuses on the off‑court response rather than on‑court performance.

articles.original_title: Stephen A. Smith Goes Off On Lebron James | - Fathom Journal

key_points: Smith questions James’ recent on‑court effectiveness.
He comments on the Lakers’ strategic choices surrounding LeBron.
The article presents opinionated analysis without new factual revelations.

key_points: Dončić is undergoing rehab for a recent injury (specific injury not detailed).
Medical staff suggest a possible return later in the season.
Progress is described as steady, with no major complications reported.

articles.title: LeBron James and Lakers Unsettled Despite 3‑1 Series Lead
articles.original_title: LeBron James and Lakers not comfortable with 3-1 series lead - Orange County Register
articles.summary: The Orange County Register notes that LeBron James and the Los Angeles Lakers expressed discomfort with holding a 3‑1 advantage in a playoff series, emphasizing the need to stay focused and avoid complacency. The team aims to maintain intensity to close out the series decisively.
key_points: Lakers lead the series 3‑1 but remain wary of a potential comeback.
LeBron highlighted the importance of continued effort and focus.
The article underscores the psychological aspect of playoff series management.
